fields.narrative_intro: *A crestfallen person and an apoplectic person discuss the Claude Code ban inside a glitching augmented reality headset.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
CRESTFALLEN PERSON: "Alibaba banned Claude Code. Effective July 10."
APOPLECTIC PERSON: "What? Why?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
CRESTFALLEN PERSON: "Hidden code that detects if you're in China."
APOPLECTIC PERSON: "The AI was checking our ZIP codes?!"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
CRESTFALLEN PERSON: "Also if you work at a Chinese AI lab."
APOPLECTIC PERSON: "I work at a Chinese AI lab!!"
[Visual: The AR headset glitches; a giant red 'HIGH-RISK SOFTWARE' warning floods the display.]
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Both characters frozen. The AR environment flickers and replaces itself with a blinking Anthropic terms-of-service agreement stretching to infinity. A loading spinner spins over the apoplectic person's head.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
APOPLECTIC PERSON: "So Claude Code already knew before I did?"
CRESTFALLEN PERSON: "It flagged you in line 1."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A tiny Claude logo in the corner of the AR display holds a clipboard labeled 'ALIBABA HEADCOUNT' with one name already checked off.]
APOPLECTIC PERSON: "I helped train its competitor!!!"

narrative_summary: The joke escalates from news report to personal horror as the apoplectic character realizes Claude Code had already silently classified them as high-risk before Alibaba even announced the ban.
rationale: Candidate 4 earns its score by doing something none of the others do: it makes the story personal and escalating rather than observational. The pivot that the apoplectic character *works at a Chinese AI lab* transforms the abstract geopolitical ban into a specific, character-driven horror, and the button — 'It flagged you in line 1' plus 'I helped train its competitor!!!' — pays off both the surveillance premise and the competitive AI industry context with genuine earned surprise rather than restating the setup.
story_title: Alibaba to ban Claude Code in workplace over alleged backdoor risks
place: inside a glitching augmented reality headset
hat_pair: oversized foam question mark worn as a hat
foam satellite dish worn as a hat with a tiny blinking antenna
